---
title: "Global Health Spending and Maternal Mortality Dashboard" 
author: "VIOLET ESSIEN"
format: 
  dashboard:
    scrolling: true
---
```{python}
#| include: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px
from itables import show
import country_converter as coco
import plotly.graph_objects as go
```
```{python}
#| include: false
health_pct = pd.read_csv("government_health_spending_of_total_gov_spending_percent.csv")
mmr_raw = pd.read_csv("mmr_ihme.csv")
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- HEALTH PCT CLEANING ---
health_pct_clean = health_pct.rename(columns={'name': 'country'})

health_pct_long = health_pct_clean.melt(
    id_vars=['country', 'geo'],
    var_name='year',
    value_name='spending'
)

health_pct_long['year'] = health_pct_long['year'].astype(int)

health_pct_long["country"] = health_pct_long["country"].replace(
    {"UAE": "United Arab Emirates"}
)
health_pct_long["country_code"] = coco.convert(health_pct_long["country"], to="ISO3")

health_pct_long['continent'] = coco.convert(names=health_pct_long['country'], to='continent')

health_pct_long_2000_2020 = health_pct_long[
    (health_pct_long['year'] >= 2000) & (health_pct_long['year'] <= 2020)
]

# ✅ Fixed: 'india' → 'India', 'United States of America' verified
health_pct_filtered = health_pct_long_2000_2020[
    health_pct_long_2000_2020['country'].isin(
        ['Germany', 'Nigeria', 'Switzerland', 'Kenya', 'India', 'United States of America', 'Italy']
    )
]
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- VALUE BOX CALCULATIONS ---
health_pct_long_2018 = health_pct_long.query("year == 2018")

highest_row = health_pct_long_2018.sort_values("spending", ascending=False).head(1).squeeze()
highest_spending_country = highest_row["country"]
highest_spending_value = round(highest_row["spending"], 1)

lowest_row = health_pct_long_2018.sort_values("spending", ascending=True).head(1).squeeze()
lowest_spending_country = lowest_row["country"]
lowest_spending_value = round(lowest_row["spending"], 1)

global_avg_spending = round(health_pct_long['spending'].mean(), 1)

avg_hlthpct = health_pct_long.groupby('continent')['spending'].mean().reset_index()
avg_hlthpct.columns = ['Continent', 'Avg Spending (%)']
avg_hlthpct['Avg Spending (%)'] = avg_hlthpct['Avg Spending (%)'].round(2)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- PAGE 1 FIGURES ---

# Choropleth map — ✅ explicit height=380 for 45% row
fig_map = px.choropleth(
    health_pct_long_2000_2020,
    locations="country_code",
    color="spending",
    hover_name="country",
    animation_frame="year",
    title="Government Health Spending of Total Government Spending (2000-2020)",
)
fig_map.update_layout(
    height=380,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10)
)

# Line chart — ✅ explicit height=280 for 35% row
fig_chart = px.line(
    health_pct_filtered,
    x='year',
    y='spending',
    color='country',
    title='Health Spending Trend (2000-2020): Selected Countries',
    labels={'country': 'Country', 'spending': 'Health Spending (%)', 'year': 'Year'}
)
fig_chart.update_layout(
    height=280,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10)
)

# Top 10 table — ✅ explicit height=250 for 35% row
top_10_spending = health_pct_long.query("year == 2019").nlargest(10, 'spending')

fig_table = go.Figure(data=[go.Table(
    header=dict(
        values=['<b>Country</b>', '<b>Health Expenditure (%)</b>'],
        fill_color='#1E90FF',
        font=dict(color='white', size=12),
        align='left'
    ),
    cells=dict(
        values=[top_10_spending.country, top_10_spending.spending.round(2)],
        fill_color='white',
        align='left'
    )
)])
fig_table.update_layout(
    height=250,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10)
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- MMR CLEANING ---
mmr_data = mmr_raw.rename(columns={'name': 'country'})

mmr_long = mmr_data.melt(
    id_vars=['country', 'geo'],
    var_name='year',
    value_name='mortality_rate'
)

mmr_long['continent'] = coco.convert(names=mmr_long['country'], to='continent')
mmr_long["country"] = mmr_long["country"].replace({"UAE": "United Arab Emirates"})
mmr_long["country_code"] = coco.convert(mmr_long["country"], to="ISO3")
mmr_long['year'] = mmr_long['year'].astype(int)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- PAGE 2 FIGURES ---

# ✅ Fixed: 'UK' → 'United Kingdom'
mmr_long_country = mmr_long[
    mmr_long['country'].isin(
        ['China', 'Nigeria', 'Japan', 'Ghana', 'United Kingdom', 'Yemen', 'New Zealand']
    )
]

# Line chart — ✅ height=400 for 50% row
fig_chart_mmr = px.line(
    mmr_long_country,
    x='year',
    y='mortality_rate',
    color='country',
    title='Maternal Mortality Rate by Country Trend',
    labels={'country': 'Country', 'mortality_rate': 'Maternal Mortality Rate', 'year': 'Year'}
)
fig_chart_mmr.update_layout(
    height=400,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10)
)

# Bubble map — ✅ height=400 for 50% row
fig_bubble_map = px.scatter_geo(
    mmr_long,
    locations="country_code",
    color="mortality_rate",
    hover_name="country",
    size="mortality_rate",
    animation_frame="year",
    projection="natural earth",
    title="Global Maternal Mortality Crisis Trend (Bubble View)",
    color_continuous_scale=px.colors.sequential.OrRd,
    size_max=30
)
fig_bubble_map.update_geos(showcountries=True, countrycolor="LightGrey")
fig_bubble_map.update_layout(
    height=400,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10)
)

# Box plot — ✅ renamed fig_box_mmr, height=380 for 50% row
fig_box_mmr = px.box(
    mmr_long.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="Maternal Mortality Distribution by Continent (2018)",
    labels={"mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Rate", "continent": "Continent"}
)
fig_box_mmr.update_layout(
    height=380,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10)
)

# MMR Table — ✅ height=350 for 50% row
top_10_mmr = mmr_long.query("year == 2019").nlargest(10, 'mortality_rate')

fig_table_mmr = go.Figure(data=[go.Table(
    header=dict(
        values=['<b>Country</b>', '<b>Mortality Rate</b>'],
        fill_color='indigo',
        font=dict(color='white', size=12),
        align='left'
    ),
    cells=dict(
        values=[top_10_mmr.country, top_10_mmr.mortality_rate.round(1)],
        fill_color='lavenderblush',
        align='left'
    )
)])
# ✅ Fixed: was updating fig_table before, now correctly updates fig_table_mmr
fig_table_mmr.update_layout(
    height=350,
    margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=10, b=10)
)
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- MERGED DATA FOR PAGE 3 ---
hlthpct_mmr = pd.merge(
    health_pct_long,
    mmr_long,
    on=['continent', 'country', 'country_code', 'year'],
    how='inner'
)

hlthpct_mmr['efficiency'] = hlthpct_mmr['spending'] / hlthpct_mmr['mortality_rate']

region_summary = hlthpct_mmr.groupby(['continent', 'year']).agg({
    'spending': 'mean',
    'mortality_rate': 'mean'
}).reset_index()
```
```{python}
#| include: false
# --- PAGE 3 FIGURES ---
# ✅ All heights set for their respective rows: 40%→350px, 30%→260px

# Bubble scatter — 40% row
fig_bubble = px.scatter(
    hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018"),
    x="spending",
    y="mortality_rate",
    size="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    hover_name="country",
    title="Health Spending vs Maternal Mortality (2018)",
    labels={"spending": "Health Spending (%)", "mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Ratio"}
)
fig_bubble.update_layout(height=350, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))

# MMR trend line — 40% row
fig_trend = px.line(
    region_summary,
    x="year",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="MMR Trends by Continent Over Time",
    labels={'continent': 'Continent', 'mortality_rate': 'Mortality Rate'}
)
fig_trend.update_layout(height=350, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))

# Spending box — 30% row  ✅ renamed from fig_box (different from MMR box)
fig_box_spending = px.box(
    hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="spending",
    color="continent",
    title="Health Spending Distribution by Continent (2018)",
    labels={"spending": "Health Spending (%)", "continent": "Continent"}
)
fig_box_spending.update_layout(height=260, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))

# Compare bar — 30% row
fig_compare = px.bar(
    region_summary.query("year == 2018"),
    x="continent",
    y="spending",
    color="continent",
    title="Average Health Spending by Continent (2018)",
    labels={'continent': 'Continent', 'spending': 'Avg Spending (%)'}
)
fig_compare.update_layout(height=260, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))

# Top spending + MMR overlay — 30% row
top_data = hlthpct_mmr.query("year == 2018").nlargest(10, "spending")
top_hlthmmr = px.bar(
    top_data,
    x="spending",
    y="country",
    color="mortality_rate",
    orientation="h",
    title="Top 10 Spending Countries with MMR Overlay (2018)",
    labels={'spending': 'Health Spending (%)', 'country': 'Country', 'mortality_rate': 'MMR'}
)
top_hlthmmr.update_layout(height=260, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))

# Scatter all years — 30% row
fig_cont = px.scatter(
    hlthpct_mmr,
    x="spending",
    y="mortality_rate",
    color="continent",
    title="Spending vs Mortality by Continent (All Years)",
    labels={"spending": "Health Spending (%)", "mortality_rate": "Maternal Mortality Ratio"}
)
fig_cont.update_layout(height=260, margin=dict(l=10, r=10, t=40, b=10))
```

# Government Health Spending Percent of Overall Expenditure

## ## Row {height=15%}

### Column {width="33%"}

::: {.valuebox icon="cash-stack" color="#1E90FF" title="Highest Health Spending 2018"}
`{python} str(highest_spending_value)`
`{python} highest_spending_country`
:::

### Column {width="33%"}

::: {.valuebox icon="thermometer" color="#87CEFA" title="Lowest Health Spending 2018"}
`{python} str(lowest_spending_value)`
`{python} lowest_spending_country`
:::

### Column {width="33%"}

::: {.valuebox icon="bar-chart-fill" color="#104160" title="Global Average Health Expenditure"}
`{python} str(global_avg_spending)`
:::
:::

## Row {height=45%}

::: {.column width="100%"}
```{python}
fig_map.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=25%}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{python}
fig_chart.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%"}
```{python}
fig_table.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=10%}

::: {.column width="100%"}
```{python}
show(avg_hlthpct)
```
:::

# Maternal Mortality Rate

## Row {height=50%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_bubble_map.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_chart_mmr.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=50%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_box_mmr.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_table_mmr.show()
```
:::

# Relationship: Health Spending vs Maternal Mortality

## Row {height=40%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_bubble.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_trend.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=30%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
fig_box_spending.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_compare.show()
```
:::

## Row {height=30%}

::: {.column width="60%"}
```{python}
top_hlthmmr.show()
```
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
```{python}
fig_cont.show()
```
:::

# About Data

## Row

::: {.column width="100%"}

### Introduction

The aim of choosing these indicators is evaluating the **Impact of Government Health Expenditure on Global Maternal Mortality**. This project serves as the capstone for the GRAPH Course, demonstrating a comprehensive end-to-end data science workflow — from environment setup and raw data ingestion to graphics and GitHub deployment.

**SDG Target 3.1** aims to reduce the global maternal mortality ratio to less than 70 per 100,000 live births by 2030. This dashboard explores: *How efficiently do different nations convert public health spending into the preservation of maternal lives?*

### Key Observations

- Health spending increases across most countries over time, especially after 2010
- **USA** is the highest spender with rapid growth post-2012
- **Nigeria** shows the most volatility — a sharp rise around 2004–2006, drop, then modest recovery by 2015
- A clear gap exists between developed (USA, Germany) and developing countries (Kenya, Nigeria)
- Lower government health spending is associated with higher maternal mortality ratios in many African countries

:::
